researcher_notes: Selectors: username + platform handles (telegram_handle, discord_id, mastodon acct). Audit ranking confirmed live (2026-06): Maigret (now v0.6.1, 2026-06-17) > Naminter > Linkook > Sherlock. Sherlock + Maigret already wired in src/tools/username_tools.py. For per-platform PROFILE EXTRACTION the cleanest structured routes are native public JSON APIs (GitHub, Reddit, Mastodon, YouTube) plus socid-extractor (URL->record, the engine behind Maigret) and instaloader for Instagram. X/Twitter and Discord have no reliable free structured route (API lockdown / token+ToS) and are gaps. Telegram profile/group enumeration needs Telethon + user API_ID/API_HASH (custom, cookie/session-class auth).

best.name: Maigret
best.notes: Best default. 3000+ sites; run --top-sites 500 not -a. Embeds socid-extractor so it pulls display name/bio/location and linked emails & account IDs from hit profiles. Already wired but current wrapper parses plain stdout, not --json ndjson; switch to ndjson to capture the email/name metadata it already produces. Pin version (deps changed across 0.5->0.6).

best.name: Naminter
best.notes: Anti-bot SECOND PASS. Async + browser impersonation (curl_cffi) to beat Cloudflare/Akamai - exactly the sites Sherlock/Maigret false-negative on. WhatsMyName-backed. JSON/CSV/PDF. Run after Maigret to recheck misses; URL-only (no metadata extraction).

runner_up.name: Sherlock
runner_up.notes: Fast, ~400 sites, no metadata. Largely redundant once Maigret runs but cheap cross-check. Already wired.

capabilities.gaps: None for breadth. Caveat: every checker false-negatives on heavily bot-protected sites; Naminter mitigates but does not fully solve. Captcha-gated platforms remain unconfirmable.

best.name: Linkook
best.notes: The pivoter. From one username, recursively discovers linked social accounts AND associated emails ACROSS DIFFERENT usernames, follows the links recursively. Cross-checks discovered emails against HudsonRock infostealer DB. Exports Neo4j-ready JSON (neo4j_export.json) - maps directly onto our graph model. This is the sock-puppet/linked-account capability the audit calls out.

best.name: socid-extractor
best.maintained: very active (soxoj; engine behind Maigret; 150+ sites, 2026)
best.notes: PROFILE URL -> structured record (display name, bio, location, linked emails, internal user IDs, cross-site links). Input is a URL not a username, so it pivots ON the hits from Maigret/Sherlock/Naminter. Pairs with enumeration to turn a list of profile URLs into identity attributes. Maigret already invokes it internally; expose standalone for re-running on URLs found by other tools.

runner_up.name: Maigret (extraction mode)
runner_up.notes: Maigret's built-in socid-extractor pass already yields linked emails/names per hit; capture via --json ndjson. Use as the always-on baseline pivot before reaching for Linkook.

capabilities.gaps: Closing the last mile to a real-world identity still needs paid APIs (OSINT Industries, Predicta, Pipl, DeHashed) - out of free/structured scope.

best.name: GitHub Users API
best.maintained: active (official GitHub REST API; 60 req/hr unauth, 5000/hr with token)
best.notes: Cleanest structured GitHub profile pull: display name, bio, company, blog/website, location, public email, follower counts, created_at. Same call pattern already used in name_tools.py name_to_username (line 338) but not exposed as a username-input profile tool. Add optional GITHUB_TOKEN env for rate limit.

best.name: OSGINT
best.install: git clone https://github.com/hippiiee/osgint && pip install -r osgint/requirements.txt
best.notes: Goes beyond the API: recovers a user's commit email via the GitHub events/commit-spoof technique (email2username and username2email), plus GPG keys and creation date. Use when the public profile email is hidden. No pip package -> clone+run.

runner_up.name: Zen
runner_up.notes: Email-from-GitHub-user/repo via commit history. OSGINT is inspired by it and is broader; keep Zen as fallback.

capabilities.gaps: None - GitHub is the best-covered platform here.

best.name: Reddit user about.json
best.maintained: active (Reddit public JSON; read-only, generous rate limit; custom User-Agent recommended)
best.notes: Public profile JSON: account creation date, karma, verified status, profile description, avatar, is_employee/is_mod flags. MUST send a custom User-Agent header (default UA gets 429). For activity, append /user/{selector}/submitted.json and /comments.json. CLAUDE.md says Reddit is already a v1/keybase-era collector - confirm registry before re-wiring.

runner_up.name: Reddit User Extractor
runner_up.notes: Bulk-pulls a user's comments/posts. Lower priority than the native about.json + submitted.json/comments.json endpoints, which need no extra dependency.

best.name: Mastodon accounts/lookup API
best.maintained: active (official Mastodon REST API; public, unauthenticated)
best.notes: For a full handle user@instance.tld: split to instance + acct. Returns id, display_name, note (bio, may contain linked sites/emails), url, created_at, followers/following counts, fields (often hold verified personal links). Note: lookup of a *remote* acct on an arbitrary instance may require the home instance; resolve instance from the handle's domain. No auth needed on the home instance.

best.name: Masto
best.notes: Searches a handle/name across Mastodon instances and pulls posts/profile. Useful when instance is unknown; otherwise the native lookup API is cleaner.

capabilities.gaps: Cross-instance discovery (which instance a bare username lives on) is weak - native API needs the instance domain. Maigret/Naminter partially cover instance discovery.

best.name: YouTube Data API v3 (channels)
best.maintained: active (official Google API; free tier 10000 units/day)
best.notes: forHandle= resolves @handles; use forUsername= for legacy usernames or id= for channel IDs. Returns title, description (often has contact email/links), country, custom URL, published date, subscriber/view counts, banner. Free but requires a Google API key (api_key auth).

runner_up.name: yt-dlp (channel metadata)
runner_up.notes: No-API-key fallback: scrapes channel name, description, uploader_url, channel_follower_count from the channel page as JSON. Use when no Google key is configured.

capabilities.gaps: Channel email is usually behind a captcha on the About tab; API description field is the realistic source.

best.name: Instaloader
best.notes: Public profile metadata (full name, bio, external_url, follower/following/post counts, profile pic, is_private/is_verified) via Python API Profile.from_username. Anonymous works for basic public profiles but Instagram now rate-limits/blocks anon hard -> treat as custom (session-class): supply a logged-in session/cookies for reliability. Library use (instaloader.Profile) is cleaner than CLI for just metadata. Expect breakage as IG changes endpoints.

runner_up.name: socid-extractor (Instagram)
runner_up.notes: Lighter pull of IG user id + basic fields from the profile URL when full instaloader session isn't available. Brittle vs IG anti-bot.

capabilities.gaps: Instagram is hostile to anonymous automation; durable extraction effectively requires authenticated cookies (sock-puppet account) -> custom/credential-vault handling.

best.name: Telescan
best.install: git clone https://github.com/pielco11/telescan && pip install -r telescan/requirements.txt
best.notes: Find which groups a user is in / users within a group, plus profile fields. REQUIRES a logged-in Telegram account: API_ID + API_HASH (from my.telegram.org) and a Telethon .session. Auth=cookies/session class -> custom runner + credential vault. Constraint: target often must be in your contacts or a shared group.

best.name: TeleTracker
best.run: python TeleTracker/TeleTracker.py  # bot token + channel/chat id; Telethon
best.notes: Channel-centric: downloads channel messages/media, extracts bot & channel metadata, logs JSON/text, tracks online status. Needs a bot token + chat/channel id. Complements Telescan (user-centric).

capabilities.gaps: No no-auth structured route for Telegram user/handle profile data. Everything credible needs Telethon + a real Telegram account (session) or a bot token. The web-search line is the better primary for raw telegram_handle per CLAUDE.md.

excluded.reason: Twitter scraper, broken by X API lockdown (audit DEAD list).
Degraded/broken by X API lockdown; unreliable for X. Audit flags X scraping as best-effort only.
Nitter instances broken/blocked by X; not a dependable structured source.
Twitter scraper, same X API-lockdown breakage; cli_research, not automatable.
X-dependent scraper, unreliable post API lockdown.
Apify actors are paid (per-run credits), no durable free tier for automation.
Apify actor, paid per-run; instaloader covers the free path.
Apify actor, paid; native reddit .json API is free.
Discord All-In-One; Discord profile extraction needs a bot/user token and violates ToS; no clean free structured route. Discord = gap.
Username checker with no native JSON output -> not pipeline-friendly; Maigret/Naminter supersede.
soxoj flags unmaintained (audit dead-tool list).
Heavy/Node-based, largely stale; redundant with Maigret/Naminter.
Nicknames tool, unmaintained, superseded by Maigret.
Nickname checker, niche/Russian-site focused; Maigret covers superset.
Redundant reimplementation of Sherlock; no metadata advantage.
web_interface (manual website), not automatable.
web_interface (manual website), X analytics, manual-only.
web_interface (manual website), manual-only.
Sock-puppet face generator, not an investigation/extraction tool (creation, not gathering).
Sock-puppet persona generator (creation utility), out of gathering scope.
